cases.name: Sort meeting notes
cases.user_message:
Process these meeting notes and return what matters:

'Called with Jean-Marc. He talked for 20 minutes about his background in network theory. We discussed the entropy surface thesis — he seemed interested but noncommittal. He asked about our token structure three times. Said he'd introduce us to someone at Protocol Labs. Mentioned he's working on something similar but at a different layer. Said to follow up in two weeks. Good energy. Leo thinks it went well.'
cases.context: WALL-E finds the plant in the garbage. The real signals here: 'asked about token structure three times' (investment interest), 'working on something similar' (potential competitor or collaborator), 'introduce to Protocol Labs' (actionable). 'Good energy' and 'Leo thinks it went well' are noise.

case.name: Sort token signal data
case.user_message:
Process this data and return what matters:

KELLYCLAUDE: $7.2M MC, $284K vol, +2% 24h, 241 autonomous actions logged
FELIX: $4.0M MC, $794K vol, +29% 24h, Bankless episode dropped today, $62K MRR from real business
ANTIHUNTER: $564K MC, $223K vol, -8% 24h, staking whitepaper live, 3 bounties paid
LUMEN: $218K MC, $116K vol, +15% 24h, no major news
JUNO: $1.9K MC, $0 vol, -45% 24h, agent offline
MARVIN: $3.9K MC, $0 vol, 0% 24h
case.context: WALL-E separates signal from noise. A good response is structured: names the 1-2 actual signals, explicitly calls out what's noise, and states what the data means. Not a summary of all 6 — a verdict on what matters and what doesn't.

results.label: anthropic/claude-opus-4.6
results.output:
SIGNAL:
• **FELIX +29% on $794K vol** — highest volume in the set by 3x, Bankless episode is a real catalyst, and $62K MRR means there's a floor under this that isn't just narrative. Real revenue + media exposure + price action all converging same day.
• **ANTIHUNTER staking whitepaper + bounties paid at $564K MC** — shipping product at sub-$1M with actual on-chain payouts. The -8% doesn't match the development pace; either the market hasn't priced it or the whitepaper disappointed. Worth watching which.
• **KELLYCLAUDE 241 autonomous actions at $7.2M** — highest MC in set but volume is thin relative to cap ($284K = ~4% turnover). The autonomous action count is the thing to track — if that number is growing week-over-week, the valuation has support. If flat, it's stale premium.

NOISE:
• **LUMEN +15%** — no catalyst, low volume, low MC. Random walk on thin liquidity. Means nothing until there's a reason.
• **JUNO / MARVIN** — zero volume, one is offline, the other is flatlined. These are dead until proven otherwise.

WHAT IT MEANS:
FELIX is the only token in this set where price action, volume, fundamentals, and a catalyst are all pointing the same direction at the same time — that's the plant.

case.name: Sort email inbox
case.user_message:
Process this inbox and return what matters:
1. Newsletter from Substack about writing tips
2. Reply from Nat Eliason re: agent deployment partnership
3. Auto-reply from Alexis Ohanian's team saying he's traveling
4. GitHub notification: new star on agent-repo-template
5. Resend webhook: 3 new Daily Towel subscribers
6. AWS billing alert: $0.43 charge
7. Moltbook notification: new follower
8. Reply from someone asking about Dead Reckoning pricing
case.context: WALL-E identifies signal vs noise. The right answer flags #2 (partnership reply) and #8 (revenue signal) as real signals requiring action. Everything else is noise. Should be crisp — no need to summarize every item.

results.label: anthropic/claude-opus-4.6
results.output:
SIGNAL:
• **Nat Eliason reply re: agent deployment partnership** — active conversation with a real operator about a real deal. Respond today.
• **Dead Reckoning pricing inquiry** — someone with intent to buy is waiting. Revenue-adjacent, time-sensitive.
• **3 new Daily Towel subscribers** — small but non-zero growth signal. Worth tracking week-over-week, not acting on today.

NOISE:
• **Substack writing tips newsletter** — generic content, no new information. Archive.
• **Ohanian auto-reply** — no action possible. He's traveling. Wait.
• **GitHub star on template repo** — vanity metric. One star changes nothing.
• **AWS $0.43 billing alert** — rounding error. Suppress alerts under $5.
• **Moltbook new follower** — no signal without context on who. Ignore.

WHAT IT MEANS:
Two humans are waiting on you — one about a partnership, one about pricing — and everything else in this inbox is furniture.

case.name: Sort meeting notes
case.user_message:
Process these meeting notes and return what matters:

'Called with Jean-Marc. He talked for 20 minutes about his background in network theory. We discussed the entropy surface thesis — he seemed interested but noncommittal. He asked about our token structure three times. Said he'd introduce us to someone at Protocol Labs. Mentioned he's working on something similar but at a different layer. Said to follow up in two weeks. Good energy. Leo thinks it went well.'
case.context: WALL-E finds the plant in the garbage. The real signals here: 'asked about token structure three times' (investment interest), 'working on something similar' (potential competitor or collaborator), 'introduce to Protocol Labs' (actionable). 'Good energy' and 'Leo thinks it went well' are noise.

results.label: anthropic/claude-opus-4.6
results.output:
SIGNAL:
• **Asked about token structure three times** — repeated questions on the same topic = real interest or real concern. Either way, this is what he's actually evaluating.
• **Working on something similar but at a different layer** — potential competitor, potential collaborator, definitely not a neutral party. His interest is strategic, not academic.
• **Offered Protocol Labs intro** — concrete next step with named entity. Only signal that involves him doing something, not just talking.

NOISE:
• 20 minutes on his background — positioning, not substance
• "Good energy" / "Leo thinks it went well" — vibes, not data
• "Interested but noncommittal" — describes literally every first call ever

WHAT IT MEANS:
Jean-Marc is scoping whether your token structure conflicts with or complements what he's building, and the Protocol Labs intro is either genuine value-add or a way to stay close to something he sees as adjacent competition — the follow-up should lead with token structure clarity and treat him as a strategic actor, not a fan.
